TAMIL NADU MEDICAL COUNCIL
Supplementary Medical Register for the year 2020


REGISTRATION FEES 
Rs. P. 
(a) For First Registration (other than reciprocal registration) under section 14 of  3,500.00/- 
      the Tamil Nadu Medical Registration Act,1914, including Stamp Fees Act 
      postage for a free copy of the Tamil Nadu Medical Register, Smart Name 
      Board and Identity card 
(b) For  Foreign Medical Graduates Permanent Registration  
 
         20,000.00/- 
      including Stamp Fees Act postage for a free copy of the  
      Tamil Nadu Medical Register, Smart Name Board and Identity card 
(c) For  the Registration of each Additional Qualification obtained –Degree   
4,000-00/- 
      subsequent to first registration including Smart Name Board  
Diploma 
3,000-00 /- 
      and Identity Card 
 
 
 
 
 
Superspeciality         5,000-00/- 
(d) For Change of Name 
 2,000.00 
(e) For Reciprocal Registration 
    Nil 
(f) For Provisional Registration 
            1,500.00/- 
(g)For Foreign Medical Graduates Provisional Eligibility  Certificate
10,000.00/- 
(h) For Provisional Registration Certificate 
           1,000.00/-  
(i) For Tamil Nadu Medical Council Certificate of Good standing                             5,000.00/- 
(j) For No Objection Certificate   & NOC Provisional
           2,000.00/- 
(k)  For C.T.Forms ,E.C.M.G.(Verification) 
           8,000.00/- 
(l)For Updation State Medical Registry obtain E Certificate 
500.00/- 
(m) For MCI /NMC Certificate of Good Standing 
            1,000.00/-       
(n) For NOC Cancellation 
1,000.00/- 
(o)   Other Miscellaneous Items in 
       (Date Extension & Change of College) 
       and Registration related Matters. 
1,000.00/- 
(p)  For Delay of  Additional Registration 
        (for every one year Rs.1,000/- as per MCI /NMC norms) 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
Public Health Qualification of a University, the Diplomas granted by Universities and the Medical and 
Public Health Qualifications granted by property constituted bodies recognised by the Central or the 
State Government may be accepted for registration as additional qualifications provided the applicant 
can be  registered  on his original qualification (Council’s) Resolution No.22 dated 6th April 1937”. 
2


 
IMPORTANT NOTICES. 
 
 
 
 
Registered Medical Practitioners should be careful to send the Registrar 
immediate Notice of any change in their registered addresses and also to answer all inquiries 
that may be  sent to them by the Registrar in regard thereto in order that their correct 
addresses may be duly inserted in the Medical Register, they should quote their Registration 
Certificate numbers with  their names in full when corresponding with the Registrar. 
 
 
No charge is made for alteration of address. 
 
A copy of the Annual Medical List wherein the name first appears after the first 
registration will be supplied gratis is to every person registered. 
After the publication of the name in the printed Medical Register, the last 
edition of the register alone is the legal evidence of registration. 
 
 
All persons registered under whatever diploma or diplomas are legally 
qualified for the practice of Medicine, Surgery and Midwifery. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
3
